mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market slack signals deteriorating consumer spending power and rising recession probability, causing equity markets to price in lower earnings and produce negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising real auto sales and declining federal funds rates indicates robust consumer demand supported by cheap credit, which directly boosts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS growth and FEDFUNDS changes yields a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary easing, positive month-over-month growth in industrial production confirms that accommodative policy is successfully transmitting to the real economy, triggering short-term momentum and positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The positive relationship between INDPRO growth and 5-day returns reverses or becomes insignificant during easing cycles, with a Sharpe ratio below 0.5 for the long-only strategy.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth directly measures aggregate economic output and corporate profit capacity, establishing a persistent positive correlation with S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 growth against 60-day forward returns falls below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows, or returns are negative in >60% of positive-GDP quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI exceeds 245.0, persistent inflationary pressure forces markets to anticipate aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in >50% of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above 245.0, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts signals synchronized strength across labor and capital investment cycles, reducing recession risk and driving positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline market-neutral strategy, with a cumulative alpha below 2% over any 24-month out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, a decline in industrial production indicates capacity constraints and supply chain bottlenecks are choking off output, prompting equity sell-offs and negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The negative signal produces positive returns in >55% of late-cycle periods, or the predictive power vanishes when tested against early-cycle expansion data.
